channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Safa congress expels four suspended NEC members after court challenge
item.description: The South African Football Association (Safa) has expelled four suspended national executive committee (NEC) members following a resolution taken during the organisation’s congress held at Birchwood H...

item.title: NSW Resolves Majority of NAFDAC and SON Permit Processing Issues After Platform Rollout
item.description: The National Single Window (NSW) management don announce say dem don resolve about 99 per cent of the 9,800 operational problems linked to licences, permits, certificates and other regulatory document...

item.title: Geraldine Chaplin’s Lasting Influence on International Art-House Cinema
item.description: The article reflects on the career and artistic legacy of Geraldine Chaplin, emphasizing her importance in both Hollywood and European cinema. As the daughter of legendary filmmaker and actor Charlie ...

item.title: Police reshuffle President Ruto’s security team after breach at Kilifi event
item.description: Inspector-General of Police Douglas Kanja has ordered an immediate reshuffle and reconstitution of President William Ruto’s security team after a security breach during a public function in Ganze, Kil...

item.title: Waymo Faces Operational Challenges as Robotaxi Expansion Continues Across U.S. Cities
item.description: This edition of TechCrunch Mobility focuses on the current state of the robotaxi industry, highlighting the gap between public perception and the operational reality faced by autonomous vehicle compan...

item.title: Western premiers gather in Kananaskis as Alberta and B.C. clash over pipeline and separatism issues
item.description: Premiers from Western Canada and the three northern territories are set to meet in Kananaskis, Alberta, for a two-day summit dominated by tensions between Alberta Premier Danielle Smith and British Co...

item.title: Benguet farmers report losses amid low vegetable prices and raise concerns over proposed carrot imports
item.description: Farmers in Benguet, known as the country’s major upland vegetable-producing region, are facing significant financial difficulties due to persistently low farmgate prices and rising production and tran...

item.title: The Age publishes its Monday Superquiz and Mini Crossword for subscribers
item.description: The Age released its regular Monday edition of the Superquiz puzzle feature on 25 May 2026, offering readers an opportunity to test their general knowledge and puzzle-solving skills. The article, writ...

item.title: Commentators criticised for omitting Taylor Swift’s name during NBA broadcast featuring Travis Kelce appearance

item.title: David Pocock open to future lower house move amid discussion of independent political organisation
item.description: Independent ACT Senator David Pocock has left the door open to a possible future shift from the Senate to the House of Representatives, while also commenting on broader discussions among independents ...

item.title: House Speaker Mike Johnson accused of blocking war powers vote related to U.S. policy on Iran
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson has been accused of abruptly removing a war powers resolution from the House floor after it appeared to have enough bipartisan support to pass. According to Representative G...

item.description: The Philippine Sports Commission (PSC) announced that medalists in the 2026 Palarong Pambansa will receive cash incentives for the first time in the history of the national student-athlete competition...

item.title: Regulators step up fight against fake and substandard medicines in South Africa
item.description: The article explores the growing problem of substandard and falsified medicines in South Africa, and how regulators are responding to an expanding black market and safety threat. It highlights the exp...

item.title: Bruce Springsteen uses his concerts to promote anti-Trump political activism and democratic resistance
item.description: The article argues that Bruce Springsteen has become one of the most prominent celebrity voices opposing Donald Trump during his recent ‘Land of Hope and Dreams’ tour across the United States. Writing...

item.title: Tanzania positions AFCON 2027 as a major driver for tourism and business opportunities
item.description: Tanzania is actively preparing to maximise the economic benefits expected from co-hosting the 2027 Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON). The country sees the tournament as a major platform for boosting touri...

item.title: Hands-on review of Amazon’s Bee AI wearable highlights productivity benefits and privacy concerns
item.description: A TechCrunch reviewer tested Bee, an AI-powered wearable wrist device recently updated after Amazon’s acquisition of the startup behind it. The gadget functions as a personal assistant that continuous...

item.title: JAMB Reports Over 22.5 Million UTME and Direct Entry Registrations Between 2015 and 2026
item.description: The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has disclosed that over 22.5 million candidates have registered for the Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME) and Direct Entry (DE) betw...

item.title: Labour unions allege delay and interference in ICPC corruption investigation at National Library of Nigeria
